channel.title: Whamcloud Community JIRA
channel.description: This file is an XML representation of an issue

item.title: [LU-19487] sanityn test_16g: timeout
item.description:
This issue was created by maloo for Alex Zhuravlev <bzzz@whamcloud.com>

This issue relates to the following test suite run: https://testing.whamcloud.com/test_sets/0177934b-cf73-4d21-bd30-2db6bcadbc1a

test_16g failed with the following error:

Timeout occurred after 245 minutes, last suite running was sanityn

Test session details:
clients: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/117592 - 4.18.0-553.76.1.el8_10.x86_64
servers: https://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/117592 - 4.18.0-553.76.1.el8_lustre.x86_64

<<Please provide additional information about the failure here>>

VVVVVVV DO NOT REMOVE LINES BELOW, Added by Maloo for auto-association VVVVVVV
sanityn test_16g - Timeout occurred after 245 minutes, last suite running was sanityn

status.description: The issue is open and ready for the assignee to start work on it.

comments.comment:
"Patrick Farrell <pfarrell@whamcloud.com>" uploaded a new patch: https://review.whamcloud.com/c/fs/lustre-release/+/64267
Subject: LU-19487 osc: return SHRINK_STOP when scan frees nothing
Project: fs/lustre-release
Branch: master
Current Patch Set: 1
Commit: f4cddaa3a51669c518b9944f2d704f14fc1a602c
NB: the build system is too broken to be trying to reproduce this now, will leave it until next time it is hit and hopefully builders are more happy.

This failed 6/1433 runs in the past 4 weeks, about 1/250 runs (normally about 22s, unless timeout is hit). Not easily reproduced, but should be hit in about 2h with testlist=sanityn env=ONLY=16g,ONLY_REPEAT=500 to confirm it can be reliably reproduced, and then see if reverting that patch stops the test failures (though that would return the original issue).

It is hit on both el8.10 (4x) and el9.5 (2x), which disproves a theory I had that it might be caused by some difference in the shinier API.

My current theory is that this is being hit during drop_caches so it is exiting too early, but the kernel really wants the slab shrunk and keeps on calling the shrinker to free some page that it never gets to, or it is passed -1 pages to scan (to catch all pages) and it is scanning too much....

The test failed for the first time on master on 2025-08-02 with patch https://review.whamcloud.com/60498 ("LU-19223 shrinkers: Add nr_scanned to all shrinkers"), and then on master-next on 2025-09-09, and then started failing intermittently on master on 2025-09-11.  While patch 60498 is not landed yet, it was based on patch https://review.whamcloud.com/60489 ("LU-19223 osc: stop after scanning") that landed on 2025-09-08, so I would guess that patch is the source of the issue as it is also in the shrink caches codepath that is showing in the stack trace.  There are numerous changes in the slab shrinker API and it is entirely possible that 

https://testing.whamcloud.com/search?horizon=15552000&status%5B%5D=TIMEOUT&test_set_script_id=570ba67a-4a46-11e0-a7f6-52540025f9af&sub_test_script_id=47191573-21df-49fd-96c0-d6033fb3f5e8&source=sub_tests#redirect

I've tried to strace it:

kill(7689, SIGUSR1)                     = 0
rt_sigaction(SIGUSR1, {sa_handler=SIG_IGN, sa_mask=[USR1], sa_flags=SA_RESTORER|SA_RESTART, sa_restorer=0x7faeb8066400}, {sa_handler=0x5638d89752b0, sa_mask=[], sa_flags=SA_RESTORER|SA_RESTART, sa_restorer=0x7faeb8c45c20}, 8) = 0
kill(0, SIGUSR1)                        = 0
--- SIGUSR1 {si_signo=SIGUSR1, si_code=SI_USER, si_pid=7688, si_uid=0} ---
kill(7689, SIGCONT)                     = 0
rt_sigaction(SIGCONT, {sa_handler=SIG_IGN, sa_mask=[CONT], sa_flags=SA_RESTORER|SA_RESTART, sa_restorer=0x7faeb8066400}, {sa_handler=SIG_DFL, sa_mask=[], sa_flags=0}, 8) = 0
kill(0, SIGCONT)                        = 0
--- SIGCONT {si_signo=SIGCONT, si_code=SI_USER, si_pid=7688, si_uid=0} ---
rt_sigreturn({mask=[HUP INT QUIT USR1 ALRM TERM CHLD]}) = -1 EINTR (Interrupted system call)
wait4(7689, 0x7ffcf1ff56bc, WNOHANG, NULL) = 0

USR1 was sent, but rw_seq_cst_vs_drop_caches didn't finish, not sure how this can happen.
It looks like this has failed about 20x since 2025-08-02 (searching back 6 months)

https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/a31600b9-35da-4757-9f94-d697256e7bdb
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/833dac1f-c79b-4999-a03b-33a639362561
https://testing.whamcloud.com/sub_tests/321aeea8-9a2f-4307-800e-6172828d6879

the test does the following:

	local duration=20
	local status

	timeout --preserve-status --signal=USR1 $duration \
		rw_seq_cst_vs_drop_caches -m $file1 $file2

i.e. timeout command should complete in 20 seconds, but it didn't. I guess something is wrong with rw_seq_cst_vs_drop_caches:

[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] task:timeout         state:S stack:0     pid:262454 ppid:262227 flags:0x00000080
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] Call Trace:
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  __schedule+0x2d1/0x870
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  schedule+0x55/0xf0
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  sigsuspend+0x45/0x80
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  __x64_sys_rt_sigsuspend+0x3e/0x70
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  do_syscall_64+0x5b/0x1a0
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  entry_SYSCALL_64_after_hwframe+0x66/0xcb
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] RIP: 0033:0x7f2180d5487d
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] Code: Unable to access opcode bytes at RIP 0x7f2180d54853.
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] RSP: 002b:00007ffe823cc6d8 EFLAGS: 00000246 ORIG_RAX: 0000000000000082
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] RAX: ffffffffffffffda RBX: 00007ffe823cc6fc RCX: 00007f2180d5487d
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] RDX: 0000000000000001 RSI: 0000000000000008 RDI: 00007ffe823cc700
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] RBP: 00007ffe823cc700 R08: 0000561bd6f73ff0 R09: 00007f2181726740
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] R10: 0000000000000000 R11: 0000000000000246 R12: 00007ffe823cc958
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] R13: 000000000000000a R14: 0000000000000000 R15: 0000000000000000
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] task:rw_seq_cst_vs_d state:R  running task     stack:0     pid:262455 ppid:262454 flags:0x00004084
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025] Call Trace:
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? osc_cache_shrink_scan+0x213/0x3e0 [osc]
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? do_shrink_slab+0x11d/0x330
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? shrink_slab+0xbe/0x2f0
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? drop_slab_node+0x58/0x90
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? drop_slab+0x38/0x70
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? drop_caches_sysctl_handler+0x70/0x80
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? proc_sys_call_handler+0x1a9/0x1c0
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? vfs_write+0xa5/0x1b0
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? ksys_write+0x4f/0xb0
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? do_syscall_64+0x5b/0x1a0
[Fri Oct 17 04:37:32 2025]  ? entry_SYSCALL_64_after_hwframe+0x66/0xcb
